subject: Physics
title: The Second Law: Entropy Rises
description: A fast breakdown of The Second Law: Entropy Rises for builders.

hook.text: Drop an ice cube in hot coffee and it melts. You'll never see the coffee spontaneously freeze an ice cube.

buildup.text: The second law says heat flows naturally from hot to cold, never the reverse, without external work.

discovery.text: Rudolf Clausius formalized this: the total entropy—disorder—of an isolated system always increases.

twist.text: This law gives time its direction. The past has less entropy; the future has more. Time's arrow is entropy.

climax.text: Refrigerators don't violate this law—they use electricity to pump heat from cold to hot. Work is required.

punchline.text: Disorder always grows. Heat always spreads. Time only moves forward.

quiz.question: What gives time its direction according to the second law?
options: Entropy always increases, creating an arrow from past to future
Gravity pulls everything forward in time
Light speed limits how fast time can reverse
